---
title: "Build PSID Family-Year Panel (2001–2021)"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(readr)
  library(arrow)
  library(glue)
  library(stringr)
  library(fs)
  library(here)
})

DATA_DIR  <- here("data_raw")
XWALK_DIR <- here("xwalk")
OUT_PATH  <- here("output", "psid_family_year_2001_latest.parquet")
WAVES     <- c(2001, 2003, 2005, 2007, 2009, 2011, 2013, 2015,
               2017, 2019, 2021)

dir_create(path_dir(OUT_PATH))
```

```{r dictionary_parsers}
# ---- Parse SPSS .sps dictionary for column positions ----
parse_sps_positions <- function(dict_path) {
  lines <- read_lines(dict_path)
  dl <- which(str_detect(lines, regex("^\\s*DATA\\s+LIST", ignore_case = TRUE)))
  if (!length(dl))
    return(tibble(var = character(), start = integer(),
                  end = integer(), width = integer(),
                  type = character()))

  after <- lines[seq(dl[1], length(lines))]
  stop_idx <- which(str_detect(
    after, regex("^\\s*VARIABLE\\s+LABELS", ignore_case = TRUE)
  ))
  if (length(stop_idx)) after <- after[seq(1, stop_idx[1] - 1)]

  after <- gsub("\\t", " ", after)
  after <- gsub("\\*.*$", "", after)
  after <- str_squish(after)
  after <- after[after != ""]
  sl <- which(str_detect(after, "^/"))
  if (length(sl)) after <- after[seq(sl[1], length(after))]

  blob <- paste(after, collapse = " ")
  m <- str_match_all(
    blob,
    "(?i)\\b([A-Za-z0-9_]+)\\s+(\\d+)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+)\\s*(\\([aA]\\))?"
  )[[1]]
  if (!nrow(m))
    return(tibble(var = character(), start = integer(),
                  end = integer(), width = integer(),
                  type = character()))

  tibble(
    var   = m[, 2],
    start = as.integer(m[, 3]),
    end   = as.integer(m[, 4]),
    type  = ifelse(!is.na(m[, 5]) & m[, 5] != "",
                   "character", "numeric")
  ) |>
    mutate(width = end - start + 1L) |>
    distinct(var, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
    arrange(start)
}

# ---- Read a fixed-width PSID data file using .sps dictionary ----
read_psid_fwf <- function(stem) {
  txt  <- path(DATA_DIR, paste0(stem, ".txt"))
  sps  <- path(DATA_DIR, paste0(stem, ".sps"))
  if (!file_exists(txt)) { warning(glue("Missing {stem}.txt")); return(tibble()) }
  if (!file_exists(sps)) { warning(glue("Missing {stem}.sps")); return(tibble()) }

  dict <- parse_sps_positions(sps)
  if (!nrow(dict)) { warning(glue("No positions in {stem}.sps")); return(tibble()) }

  col_types <- paste(ifelse(dict$type == "character", "c", "d"),
                     collapse = "")
  readr::read_fwf(
    file          = txt,
    col_positions = readr::fwf_positions(dict$start, dict$end, dict$var),
    col_types     = col_types,
    na            = c("", " ", ".", "NA")
  )
}
```

```{r crosswalk_loader}
# ---- Load and validate crosswalk CSVs ----
load_xwalk <- function(fname) {
  p <- path(XWALK_DIR, fname)
  if (!file_exists(p)) {
    warning(glue("Crosswalk not found: {fname}"))
    return(tibble(wave = integer(), varname = character(),
                  source = character()))
  }
  out <- read_csv(p, show_col_types = FALSE)
  # Keep only the 3 required columns (ignore extras)
  names(out) <- tolower(trimws(names(out)))
  out <- out |> select(any_of(c("wave", "varname", "source")))
  needed <- c("wave", "varname", "source")
  if (!all(needed %in% names(out))) {
    warning(glue("{fname}: missing columns {paste(setdiff(needed, names(out)), collapse=', ')}"))
    return(tibble(wave = integer(), varname = character(),
                  source = character()))
  }
  out <- out |>
    mutate(wave = as.integer(wave)) |>
    filter(!is.na(source), source != "")

  # Check for duplicate (wave, varname) — would corrupt column names
  dups <- out |> count(wave, varname) |> filter(n > 1)
  if (nrow(dups)) {
    warning(glue(
      "{fname}: duplicate (wave, varname) entries — keeping first:\n",
      paste(capture.output(print(dups)), collapse = "\n")
    ))
    out <- out |> distinct(wave, varname, .keep_all = TRUE)
  }

  # Check for duplicate (wave, source) — same ER code for two names

  dup_src <- out |> count(wave, source) |> filter(n > 1)
  if (nrow(dup_src)) {
    warning(glue(
      "{fname}: duplicate (wave, source) entries — keeping first:\n",
      paste(capture.output(print(dup_src)), collapse = "\n")
    ))
    out <- out |> distinct(wave, source, .keep_all = TRUE)
  }

  out
}

# Load all crosswalks
family_xw <- load_xwalk("family_vars_crosswalk.csv")
hs_xw     <- load_xwalk("head_spouse_crosswalk.csv")
phil_xw   <- load_xwalk("philanthropy_crosswalk.csv")
mob_xw    <- load_xwalk("mobility_crosswalk.csv")
state_xw  <- load_xwalk("state_crosswalk.csv")
relig_xw  <- load_xwalk("religiosity_crosswalk.csv")

cat("Crosswalks loaded.\n")
```

```{r apply_xwalk_fn}
# ---- Map raw ER codes → human-readable names via crosswalk ----
# Returns a tibble with renamed columns, or empty tibble if
# no matching columns found. Logs what's missing.
apply_xwalk <- function(df, xw, yr, label = "") {
  if (!nrow(df) || !nrow(xw)) return(tibble())

  xwy <- xw |> filter(wave == yr)
  if (!nrow(xwy)) return(tibble())

  found   <- xwy |> filter(source %in% names(df))
  missing <- xwy |> filter(!source %in% names(df))

  if (nrow(missing) && nchar(label)) {
    cat(glue("  {label} wave {yr}: missing {nrow(missing)} vars: ",
             "{paste(missing$source, collapse=', ')}\n\n"))
  }

  if (!nrow(found)) return(tibble())

  # Select columns in the order they appear in the crosswalk
  out <- select(df, all_of(found$source))
  names(out) <- found$varname
  out
}
```

```{r build_wave_fn}
# ---- Build a single wave of the panel ----
build_wave <- function(yr) {
  cat(glue("=== Wave {yr} ===\n"))
  fam <- read_psid_fwf(glue("FAM{yr}ER"))
  if (!nrow(fam)) return(tibble())

  # 1. Get the 1968 Family ID, interview number, year, and weight
  id68_src <- family_xw |>
    filter(wave == yr, varname == "id_1968") |>
    pull(source) |>
    first()
  fid_src <- family_xw |>
    filter(wave == yr, varname == "family_id") |>
    pull(source) |>
    first()
  iyr_src <- family_xw |>
    filter(wave == yr, varname == "interview_year") |>
    pull(source) |>
    first()
  wgt_src <- family_xw |>
    filter(wave == yr, varname == "family_weight") |>
    pull(source) |>
    first()

  if (is.na(id68_src) || !id68_src %in% names(fam))
    stop(glue("Wave {yr}: no id_1968 mapping"))
  if (is.na(fid_src) || !fid_src %in% names(fam))
    stop(glue("Wave {yr}: no family_id mapping"))
  if (is.na(iyr_src) || !iyr_src %in% names(fam))
    stop(glue("Wave {yr}: no interview_year mapping"))
  if (is.na(wgt_src) || !wgt_src %in% names(fam))
    warning(glue("Wave {yr}: no family_weight mapping — weight will be NA"))

  keys <- tibble(
    id_1968        = as.integer(fam[[id68_src]]),
    family_id      = as.integer(fam[[fid_src]]),
    interview_year = as.integer(fam[[iyr_src]]),
    wave           = as.integer(yr),
    family_weight  = if (!is.na(wgt_src) && wgt_src %in% names(fam))
                       fam[[wgt_src]] else NA_real_
  )

  # 2. Apply each crosswalk domain
  safe_add <- function(base, piece) {
    if (is.null(piece) || !ncol(piece)) return(base)
    # Guard against duplicate column names from overlapping xwalks
    dupes <- intersect(names(base), names(piece))
    if (length(dupes)) piece <- piece |> select(-all_of(dupes))
    bind_cols(base, piece)
  }

  out <- keys |>
    safe_add(apply_xwalk(fam, hs_xw,    yr, "head_spouse")) |>
    safe_add(apply_xwalk(fam, phil_xw,  yr, "philanthropy")) |>
    safe_add(apply_xwalk(fam, mob_xw,   yr, "mobility")) |>
    safe_add(apply_xwalk(fam, state_xw, yr, "state")) |>
    safe_add(apply_xwalk(fam, relig_xw, yr, "religiosity"))

  # 3. Validate: one row per family per wave
  #    Use id_1968 as the primary key — but note that splitoff families
  #    share the same id_1968, so we check (id_1968, family_id) uniqueness.
  n_dup <- sum(duplicated(out$family_id))
  if (n_dup > 0)
    stop(glue("Wave {yr}: {n_dup} duplicate family_id rows"))

  cat(glue("  {nrow(out)} families, {ncol(out)} columns\n\n"))
  out
}
```

```{r run_all_waves}
# ---- Build panel across all waves ----
panel <- map_dfr(WAVES, build_wave)

cat("\n=== PANEL SUMMARY (all families, including splitoffs) ===\n")
cat("Total rows:", nrow(panel), "\n")
cat("Total columns:", ncol(panel), "\n")
cat("Unique id_1968 values:", n_distinct(panel$id_1968), "\n")
cat("Unique family_id:", n_distinct(panel$family_id), "\n")
cat("Waves:", paste(sort(unique(panel$wave)), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

# Splitoff summary (informational only — we keep all families)
splitoff_counts <- panel |>
  group_by(id_1968, wave) |>
  summarise(n_fams = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  filter(n_fams > 1)
cat("Splitoff lineages (id_1968 with >1 family in a wave):",
    n_distinct(splitoff_counts$id_1968), "\n")
cat("Total splitoff rows:", sum(splitoff_counts$n_fams), "\n\n")

# Weight summary
cat("Family weight summary:\n")
cat("  Non-zero weights:", sum(panel$family_weight > 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  Zero weights:", sum(panel$family_weight == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  NA weights:", sum(is.na(panel$family_weight)), "\n\n")

# Distribution of waves per family_id
wpf <- table(table(panel$family_id))
cat("Waves-per-family_id distribution:\n")
print(wpf)
```

```{r validate}
# ---- Validate the panel ----

# 1. Check family_id uniqueness within wave (must always hold)
dups_fid <- panel |> count(family_id, wave) |> filter(n > 1)
if (nrow(dups_fid)) {
  print(dups_fid, n = 20)
  stop("Panel has duplicate (family_id, wave) rows.")
} else {
  cat("OK: No duplicate (family_id, wave) rows.\n")
}

# Note: id_1968 can repeat within a wave (splitoffs) — that's expected
dups_68 <- panel |> count(id_1968, wave) |> filter(n > 1)
cat("INFO:", nrow(dups_68), "id_1968 values appear >1 time in a wave",
    "(splitoffs — expected).\n")

# 2. No missing keys
cat("Missing id_1968:", sum(is.na(panel$id_1968)), "\n")
cat("Missing family_id:", sum(is.na(panel$family_id)), "\n")
cat("Missing wave:", sum(is.na(panel$wave)), "\n")
cat("Missing interview_year:",
    sum(is.na(panel$interview_year)), "\n\n")

# 3. Check that key analysis variables are present
expected_vars <- c(
  "id_1968", "family_id", "wave", "interview_year",
  "family_weight",
  "head_age", "Religious_Preference",
  "WHY MOVED 1ST",
  "DOLLAR AMT OF RELIGIOUS DONATIONS",
  "DOLLAR AMT OF NEEDY DONATIONS",
  "DOLLAR AMT OF HEALTH DONATIONS",
  "DOLLAR AMT OF EDUCATION DONATIONS",
  "Current State"
)
present <- expected_vars[expected_vars %in% names(panel)]
absent  <- expected_vars[!expected_vars %in% names(panel)]
cat("Analysis variables present:", length(present), "/",
    length(expected_vars), "\n")
if (length(absent))
  cat("  MISSING:", paste(absent, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# 4. Check no suffixed duplicates (the old bug)
suffixed <- names(panel)[str_detect(names(panel), "\\.\\.\\.[0-9]+$")]
if (length(suffixed)) {
  cat("\nWARNING: Suffixed column names detected ",
      "(likely crosswalk duplicates):\n")
  cat(" ", paste(suffixed, collapse = "\n  "), "\n")
} else {
  cat("OK: No suffixed duplicate columns.\n")
}

# 5. Spot-check dollar amounts for sentinel codes
dollar_cols <- names(panel)[str_detect(names(panel), "DOLLAR AMT")]
for (dc in dollar_cols) {
  n_sentinel <- sum(panel[[dc]] >= 999997, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (n_sentinel > 0)
    cat(glue("  NOTE: {dc} has {n_sentinel} sentinel values ",
             "(>=999997) — clean in analysis\n\n"))
}

cat("\nValidation complete.\n")
```

```{r write_output}
# ---- Write the panel ----
panel <- panel |>
  filter(!is.na(id_1968), !is.na(wave)) |>
  arrange(id_1968, family_id, wave) |>
  relocate(id_1968, family_id, interview_year, wave, family_weight)

write_parquet(panel, OUT_PATH)
cat(glue("Wrote: {OUT_PATH}\n"))
cat(glue("  {nrow(panel)} rows × {ncol(panel)} columns\n"))
cat(glue("  Waves: {paste(sort(unique(panel$wave)),
         collapse=', ')}\n"))
```
